name: Maple Caramel Apples
recipeIngredient: 10 Granny Smith or other tart apples -- chilled
recipeInstructions:
1. In a deep, heavy saucepan combine the brown sugar, maple syrup, and cream.
2. Cook the mixture over moderate heat, stirring frequently and washing down any sugar crystals clinging to the sides of the pan with a brush dipped in cold water, until the sugar is completely dissolved.
3. Bring the mixture to a boil and continue boiling it undisturbed until a candy thermometer registers 250 degrees F (121 degrees C).
4. Remove the pan from the heat, add the butter, and swirl the pan gently until the butter is melted.
5. Let the maple caramel cool for 5 minutes.
6. Push a wooden skewer or chopstick through the length of each apple and hold the apples by the skewers.
7. Dip each apple one at a time into the maple caramel, coating them well and letting the excess drip off.
8. Place the apples on buttered baking sheets until the maple caramel is cool and firm.
9. Serve the apples immediately.
Yield: 10 caramel apples
